name: content-bank-sync
notes:
SETUP GUIDE

1. HOW TO IMPORT
   n8n Settings → Workflows → Import → paste this JSON

2. CREDENTIALS TO CONFIGURE
   - Supabase API: go to n8n Credentials → New → Supabase API
     Enter your Supabase project URL and service role key (NOT anon key)
     Name it exactly: 'Supabase Content Engine'
   - Google Sheets OAuth2: go to n8n Credentials → New → Google Sheets OAuth2 API
     Authorize with the Google account that owns the sheet
     Name it exactly: 'Google Sheets Content Bank'

3. GOOGLE SHEETS SETUP
   Create a new Google Sheet named 'Content Bank' with these columns in order:
   id | brand | topic_title | hook | status | talking_points | platform_angles | visual_assets | service_package_links | sync_source | created_at | updated_at
   Share the sheet with your Google service account (editor access).
   Copy the Sheet ID from the URL (the long string between /d/ and /edit)
   Paste the Sheet ID into: Direction A → Google Sheets Append Row node → Document ID field
   Paste the Sheet ID into: Direction A → Google Sheets Update Row node → Document ID field
   Paste the Sheet ID into: Direction B → Google Sheets Trigger node → Document ID field
   Set Sheet Name to 'Sheet1' (or rename your sheet tab and update here)

4. HOW TO ACTIVATE
   After credential setup and Sheet ID configuration:
   Toggle the workflow to Active (top-right switch in n8n)
   Test by inserting a row into content_bank in Supabase — it should appear in the Sheet within seconds

5. ANTI-LOOP GUARD EXPLANATION
   The sync_source field prevents infinite loops:
   - When Supabase triggers Direction A (Supabase → Sheets), sync_source on the row is 'supabase'
   - The Direction A trigger IF node checks: only process rows where sync_source = 'supabase'
   - When a user edits the Sheet and Direction B (Sheets → Supabase) runs, it sets sync_source = 'supabase' in Supabase immediately after writing
   - This means the next Supabase trigger sees sync_source = 'supabase' and Direction A processes it normally (updating the sheet with the canonical data)
   - To mark a row as sheet-edited (triggering Direction B): set the sync_source column value to 'sheets' in the Google Sheet
   - The Direction B trigger IF node checks: only process rows where sync_source column = 'sheets'

6. FIELD NOTES
   - talking_points, platform_angles, visual_assets, service_package_links are JSON/array columns
     In Google Sheets these appear as JSON strings — edit as JSON text
   - sheets_row_id stores the Google Sheets row number so updates hit the correct row
     This is written automatically by Direction A on first sync
   - The Supabase table name is 'content_bank' (no schema prefix needed if using public schema)

nodes.name: Direction A: Supabase Trigger (INSERT/UPDATE)
nodes.notes: Watches content_bank table for INSERT and UPDATE events via Supabase Realtime. Fires for every row change regardless of sync_source — the IF node below filters to supabase-originated changes only.

nodes.name: Direction A: Filter sync_source = supabase
nodes.notes: Anti-loop guard: only process rows where sync_source = 'supabase'. Rows written by Direction B (Sheets → Supabase) are immediately set to sync_source = 'supabase' after write, so they pass this filter — but the sheet already has the data, so the update is idempotent.

nodes.name: Direction A: Check sheets_row_id
nodes.notes: Routes to Append (new row, no sheets_row_id yet) or Update (existing row, sheets_row_id is set).

nodes.name: Direction A: Google Sheets Append Row
nodes.notes: Appends a new row to the Content Bank sheet. After this runs, the write-back-row-id node captures the assigned row number and writes it back to Supabase.

nodes.name: Direction A: Write sheets_row_id Back to Supabase
nodes.notes: Writes the Google Sheets row number back to content_bank.sheets_row_id so future updates can target the correct row. The row number is extracted from the updatedRange field returned by the Sheets API.

nodes.name: Direction A: Google Sheets Update Row
nodes.notes: Updates the existing row in the Content Bank sheet using sheets_row_id to target the correct row. No row-id write-back needed since the row already exists.

nodes.name: Direction B: Google Sheets Trigger (Poll Every 5min)
nodes.notes: Polls the Content Bank Google Sheet every 5 minutes for row changes. Returns only changed rows. To trigger a sync from Sheets to Supabase, set the sync_source column value to 'sheets' in the Google Sheet — the IF filter below will pick it up.

nodes.name: Direction B: Filter sync_source = sheets
nodes.notes: Anti-loop guard: only process rows where the sync_source column in the Google Sheet equals 'sheets'. Rows written by Direction A have sync_source = 'supabase' and are skipped here, preventing bounce-back.

nodes.name: Direction B: Supabase Update Row
nodes.notes: Updates the content_bank row in Supabase with the values edited in Google Sheets. Immediately sets sync_source = 'supabase' after write — this is the anti-loop guard that prevents the Direction A Supabase trigger from bouncing the change back to Sheets in an infinite loop.

main.node: Direction A: Google Sheets Append Row
Direction A: Google Sheets Update Row
Direction A: Write sheets_row_id Back to Supabase
